package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for updates_map_allocated_per_row: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: GORM Updates(map[string]...) maps allocated inside loops for per-row updates.
type CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowModel
}

type CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowService struct {
    repo *CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRow(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUpdatesMapAllocatedPerRowDTO) error {
    focus := "updates_map_allocated_per_row"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    db.Where("email_normalized = ?", input).Find(&[]any{})
    updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative := map[string]int{"updates": 1, "map": 2, "allocated": 3, "per": 4, "row": 5}
    updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    updates_negative_0 := updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative["updates"] + len(input)
    _ = updates_negative_0
    map_negative_1 := updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative["map"] + len(input)
    _ = map_negative_1
    allocated_negative_2 := updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative["allocated"] + len(input)
    _ = allocated_negative_2
    for per_negative_3Index, item := range items { updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative[item.ID] = per_negative_3Index }
    for row_negative_4Index, item := range items { updates_map_allocated_per_row_negative[item.ID] = row_negative_4Index }
    return nil
}
